$comment: Pre-fill for PulseMCP server registration (Task 3.30). DRAFT — DO NOT SUBMIT until Phase 5 launch. PulseMCP's primary discovery path is automated scraping of npm packages with the `mcp` keyword (per the distribution doc: 'PulseMCP scrapes daily from npm with the mcp keyword'). They also accept manual submissions via https://www.pulsemcp.com/submit which require the fields below. Confirm the exact submission form at submission time.
name: peek
short_description: Bring your real authenticated browser session to AI coding agents. Local-first MCP server + Chrome extension.
long_description:
peek lets AI coding assistants see (and optionally drive) the user's actual logged-in browser. A Chrome MV3 extension records the session (rrweb DOM + console + network metadata, with PII masking) and ships it through a native-messaging stdio bridge to a local MCP server (`peek-mcp`). All data stays on the user's machine — sessions are persisted to a local SQLite database at `~/.peek/sessions.db`. Per-origin opt-in, five permission levels (Off → YOLO), and a destructive-action blocklist gate every action the agent can take.

Use cases: debugging a flaky login flow with Claude Code, asking Cursor to reproduce a bug a teammate saw, having Cline scaffold a Playwright test from a session you just recorded, letting Windsurf walk through an admin dashboard you have access to but the agent does not.

See the privacy policy at docs/peek/PRIVACY_POLICY.md.

install_instructions.extension: Install the companion Chrome extension via the Chrome Web Store (link added at Phase 5 launch) — peek-mcp requires it to capture sessions.
